channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Springbok Prop Asenathi Ntlabakanye Suspended for 18 Months Following Doping Tribunal Ruling
item.description: Springbok prop Asenathi Ntlabakanye has been handed an 18-month ban after admitting to the use of an illegal substance. The ruling, effective from 13 May 2026, follows an adverse test showing traces o...

item.title: NSW Blues secure 2026 Women’s State of Origin series with strong defence
item.description: NSW Blues have claimed back-to-back Women’s State of Origin titles after a thrilling 14-10 win over the Queensland Maroons at Suncorp Stadium. The match was tightly contested, with both teams exchangi...

item.title: Student passes away amid meningitis outbreak in Oxfordshire
item.description: A tragic incident has occurred in Oxfordshire where a student has died following a meningitis outbreak. The local health authorities have confirmed that several students were affected, and emergency m...

item.title: Ramaphosa removes Tolashe as Social Development Minister amid accountability concerns
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has dismissed Sisisi Tolashe from her position as Minister of Social Development, a move that has garnered widespread political commentary. The African National Congress (ANC...

item.title: President Zardari awards military honours to martyred and serving officers
item.description: On Thursday, President Asif Ali Zardari presided over a distinguished investiture ceremony at Aiwan-e-Sadr, conferring 47 military awards on officers and soldiers of Pakistan’s armed forces. The cerem...

item.title: Election Commission launches third phase of Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls across 16 states and 3 UTs
item.description: The Election Commission of India has announced the third phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls, covering 16 states and three Union Territories. This brings the voter list re...

item.title: Vice Ganda Collaborates with “It’s Showtime” Writers for Improvised Comedy
item.description: ABS-CBN continues to innovate in Philippine television as comedian Vice Ganda takes on a unique approach to comedy by working directly with the writers of the popular variety show “It’s Showtime.” The...

item.title: Francesca Annis: A Retrospective of Her Iconic Career and 1960s–70s Portraits
item.description: Francesca Annis stands as a timeless figure in British theatre and cinema, celebrated for a career spanning more than six decades. She first captured international attention as Lady Macbeth in Roman P...

item.title: Labour Leadership Uncertainty Intensifies Following Streeting Resignation and Burnham Speculation
item.description: The future of Labour leadership has become increasingly uncertain after Wes Streeting resigned as health secretary, criticising a lack of vision and direction under Sir Keir Starmer. Speculation has g...

item.title: Australia names full-time Counter-Terrorism Coordinator following anti-Semitism Royal Commission
item.description: The Australian government has appointed Brendan Dowling as a full-time Counter-Terrorism Coordinator within the Home Affairs Department, responding to recommendations from the anti-Semitism Royal Comm...

item.title: Sugarcane Farms in Visayas Face Early RSSI Infestation Amid Hot Weather
item.description: Sugarcane plantations across the Visayas region are experiencing a renewed infestation of the red stripe soft scale insect (RSSI), affecting around 1,080 hectares. The Sugar Regulatory Administration ...

item.title: Israeli Tourists Attack Shop in Siargao After Seeing Pro-Palestinian Flag
item.description: The Department of Tourism (DOT) has come out in defense of business owners in Siargao Island, General Luna, Surigao del Norte, after two Israeli tourists assaulted a local shop over the weekend. Accor...

item.title: Leaked recording reveals Tolashe limiting responses amid multiple allegations
item.description: A leaked audio recording has surfaced showing Minister of Social Development and ANC Women's League president Sisisi Tolashe instructing ANC MPs on how to handle questions in parliament, refusing to a...

item.title: Police uncover syndicate allegedly taking out insurance on relatives before killing them for payouts
item.description: Police in Polokwane have revealed the alleged activities of a criminal syndicate accused of killing relatives shortly after taking out insurance policies on them, allegedly to claim payouts totalling ...

item.title: US and China Discuss International AI Safety Protocols Amid Technology Race
item.description: The Washington Post's AI & Tech Brief highlights ongoing discussions between the U.S. and China regarding international safety standards for artificial intelligence. President Donald Trump’s administr...

item.title: Honda suspends $15-billion Ontario EV project amid financial and strategic challenges
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney described Honda Canada’s announcement to indefinitely suspend its $15-billion electric vehicle project in Alliston, Ontario, as a disappointing development. The decision ref...

item.title: Venice Biennale 2025: Protests, political controversy and curatorial tensions
item.description: The 61st Venice Biennale, titled In Minor Keys and curated posthumously by the late Koyo Kouoh, has been overshadowed by significant political controversies. Russia’s return after its exclusion follow...

item.title: Jaishankar Reaffirms India’s Support for Two-State Solution at BRICS Foreign Ministers’ Meeting
item.description: On May 14, 2026, External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar presented India’s official stance at the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ meeting held at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. Highlighting India’s longstanding...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Anthony Perkins in Orson Welles’ The Trial (1962)
item.description: On the set of Orson Welles’ 1962 adaptation of Franz Kafka’s The Trial, Anthony Perkins experienced what he later described as his 'greatest professional pride'. Behind-the-scenes photographs by Nicol...

item.title: Government Nears Cabinet Approval for Environmental Law Amendments
item.description: The Tanzanian government is moving fast with changes to the Environmental Management Act, Chapter 191, aiming to boost environmental governance and make institutions overseeing the environment more ef...
